product_segments.name: U.S. Commercial
product_segments.revenue: Guidance for at least $3.224B in FY2026

management_guidance.context: Raised from previous range of $7.18B, implies ~71% YoY growth
Implies at least 120% YoY growth for that segment
Reiterated commitment to GAAP net income profitability each quarter
Q1 2026 achieved, mentioned as a key metric

competitive_position.moat: Mission-critical AI operating system for real-time decision making and workflow deployment, deeply integrated into government and enterprise operations.
competitive_position.threats: High valuation and dependency on government contracts; meeting elevated market expectations for sustained hypergrowth.

key_facts.fact: Full-year 2026 revenue guidance raised to $7.65B-$7.66B, implying ~71% annual growth.
U.S. commercial revenue grew over 120% in Q1 2026; guidance for FY2026 U.S. commercial revenue at least $3.224B.
key_facts.caveat: Guidance is for full year, not ARR

key_facts.fact: Palantir achieved Rule of 40 score of 145%, comparing itself to NVIDIA and Microsoft.
key_facts.source: Q1 2026 earnings press release / Yahoo Finance
key_facts.caveat: Rule of 40 is a metric combining revenue growth and profit margin

key_facts.fact: Remaining performance obligations (RPO) were $4.5B as of March 31, 2026.
key_facts.caveat: RPO includes both recognized and unrecognized revenue from contracts

key_facts.fact: Closed 206 deals worth at least $1 million, and 72 deals worth at least $5 million in Q1 2026.
key_facts.caveat: These are deal counts with minimum values, not total contract value

analyst_thesis.bull_case: Palantir is becoming a mission-critical AI operating system with 85% revenue growth, Rule of 40 at 145%, and booming U.S. commercial demand (+120% growth). It is compared to AI infrastructure giants like NVIDIA.
analyst_thesis.bear_case: High valuation (market cap $328B) raises concerns about sustainability; business model fragility due to reliance on government contracts and potential slowdown in AI adoption growth.

key_institutions.rating: No explicit rating; mentions media sentiment score of 0.91 for PLTR vs 1.85 for META
key_institutions.thesis: PLTR has strong media sentiment but lower than Meta.
Industry comparison notes PLTR's high valuation vs peers.

data_quality.notes: Revenue segment data available from StockAnalysis LTM table; EDGAR XBRL provides total revenue only. Guidance and key facts sourced from multiple earnings transcripts and press releases.
